Sagada.location: Sagada-Besao Road, Sagada, Sagada, Mountain Province

Sagada.name: Lumiang Burial Cave
Sagada.description: A cave mouth filled with over 100 stacked pine coffins of Kankana-ey ancestors, some estimated to be over 500 years old. The cave can be connected to Sumaguing in a longer spelunking adventure.
Sagada.location: Cave Connection Trail, Ambasing, Mountain Province

Sagada.name: Sumaguing Cave Entrance
Sagada.description: The entrance to Sumaguing Cave, Sagada's most impressive limestone cavern. Also called "Big Cave," it features extraordinary stalagmite and stalactite formations and requires spelunking through underground pools — one of the best cave experiences in the Philippines.
Sagada.location: Cave Connection Trail, Ambasing, Mountain Province

Sagada.name: Sagada Pottery
Sagada.description: A workshop and showroom featuring handmade pottery crafted by Sagada's weavers and artisans, using local clay and traditional techniques. A great place to find unique handcrafted souvenirs.
Sagada.location: Sagada-Besao Road, Sagada, Sagada, Mountain Province

Sagada.name: Sunset Viewpoint
Sagada.description: A hilltop clearing above Sagada offering panoramic sunset views over the pine-covered Cordillera mountains — a peaceful spot to end the day with a cup of Sagada coffee.
Sagada.location: Sagada-Besao Road, Sagada, Sagada, Mountain Province

Sagada.name: Reverend John Armitage Staunton Monument
Sagada.description: A historical marker or monument in Sagada commemorating a significant figure or event in Philippine history — a quiet place of remembrance within the local landscape.
Sagada.location: Reverend John A. Staunton Road, Sagada, Sagada, Mountain Province

Sagada.name: Kiltepan Peak
Sagada.description: Sagada's most famous sunrise viewpoint, where on clear mornings a sea of clouds fills the valley below while mountain peaks rise above — a breathtaking spectacle that has made this one of the most shared images of the Philippines.
Sagada.location: Reverend John A. Staunton Road, Sagada, Sagada, Mountain Province

Sagada.name: Pongas Falls
Sagada.description: A waterfall accessible from Sagada via a forested hike through terraced farmland and pine woodland. The falls are a refreshing reward at the end of a trail through the scenic Cordillera landscape.

Sagada.name: Hanging Coffins
Sagada.description: One of the most striking sights in the Philippines — ancient coffins of the Igorot dead are attached to the sheer limestone cliffs above Echo Valley in Sagada. The Kankana-ey people believe the higher the coffin, the closer to ancestral spirits.
Sagada.location: Sagada Mission Compound, Sagada, Sagada, Mountain Province

Sagada.description: A historical marker or monument in Sagada commemorating a significant figure or event in Philippine history — a quiet place of remembrance within the local landscape.
Sagada.location: Sagada Mission Compound, Sagada, Sagada, Mountain Province

Sagada.name: Masferré Photographs

Sagada.location: Poblacion-Aguid Road, Sagada, Sagada, Mountain Province

Vigan.name: Burgos Museum
